data.title: Why Is Vanguard Total Stock Market (VTI) ETF Down Today, 4/23/26?
data.description:
The Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI), which tracks the broader U.S. market, slipped about 0.14% at Thursday's open as rising geopolitical tensions weighed on sentiment. Stocks came under pressure as oil prices climbed for a fourth straight day, with Brent crude (CM:BZ) moving back above $102 per barrel. The gains in oil followed stalled talks between the U.S. and Iran, with both sides failing to resume negotiations despite an extended truce.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

Market sentiment was also weighed down by Tesla's (TSLA) earnings, which sent the stock down about 4%.

VTI ETF remains up 3% over the past five days. Meanwhile, its three-month average trading volume stands at around 6.14 million shares.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: TLRY, CGC: Cannabis Stocks Soar as U.S. Finally Delivers Change in Marijuana Policy
data.description:
Shares in cannabis companies Tilray Brands (TLRY) and Canopy Growth (CGC) jumped early Thursday. This came after the U.S. government finally moved to reclassify state-legal marijuana as a less dangerous drug, signaling long-awaited action on federal cannabis policy.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The U.S. Department of Justice on Thursday announced that it has placed drug products containing marijuana and medical marijuana products under Schedule III of the U.S. Controlled Substances Act.

That moves the cannabis compounds responsible for the feeling of "highness" out of Schedule I — which is where you find drugs such as heroin and LSD, which are considered highly addictive with a strong risk of abuse. By contrast, Schedule III is reserved for substances considered to have recognized medical use and reduced potential for abuse.

Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, who announced the reclassification, noted that the move is targeted at "strengthening medical research while maintaining strict federal controls."

"This rescheduling action allows for research on the safety and efficacy of this substance, ultimately providing patients with better care and doctors with more reliable information," Blanche added.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Top BMO Analyst Lifts Alphabet Stock Price Target Ahead of Q1 Earnings, Says 'GOOGL Remains the Best Way to Own AI'
data.description:
Google-parent Alphabet (GOOGL) is scheduled to announce its Q1 earnings on April 29. GOOGL stock has risen 124% over the past year, driven by the strength in the company's cloud business, demand for its TPU (tensor processing unit) chips, and strategic AI deals. Ahead of the results, top BMO Capital analyst Brian Pitz reiterated a Buy rating on GOOGL stock and raised his price target to $410 from $400, saying "We maintain that Top Pick GOOGL remains the best way to own AI in our coverage universe, as it continues to expand its leadership positions across the AI stack."

data.title: Trump Orders U.S. Navy to 'Shoot and Kill' Mine-Laying Boats in Hormuz
data.description:
President Trump has ordered the U.S. Navy to target any boats laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. The waterway, which handles roughly 20% of global oil flows, has remained at a near standstill since the beginning of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28, creating a widespread supply shock.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

"There is to be no hesitation," Trump said in a Truth Social post. "Additionally, our mine 'sweepers' are clearing the Strait right now."

U.S., Iran Remain at Odds Over Blockade and Negotiations

Iran has communicated that it will continue to keep Hormuz closed until the U.S. ceases its blockade on ships entering and exiting Iranian ports. Lifting the blockade was also one of the conditions that Iran requested before engaging in peace talks.

In a separate post, Trump reiterated that the U.S. has "total control" over Hormuz, adding that the blockade will remain in place until Iran is able to reach a deal. Earlier this week, he extended the ceasefire between the two sides without specifying the duration.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Is Super Micro Stock (SMCI) Tumbling Today, 4/23/26?
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) is under pressure today after an independent research firm, BlueFin Research, reported that the company may have lost a major AI infrastructure deal with Oracle (ORCL). The stock fell 6% in early trading as investors reacted to the potential revenue hit.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

Report Points to Major Contract Loss

According to the report, Oracle has scrapped plans to purchase between 300 and 400 AI racks from Super Micro. Each rack, powered by Nvidia (NVDA) chips, is estimated to cost around $3.5 million, putting the total value of the canceled deal between $1.1 billion and $1.4 billion.

Market sources believe Oracle may be shifting this business to competitors like Wiwynn, Dell Technologies (DELL), and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), which are seeing increased interest as some customers pull back from Super Micro amid regulatory concerns.

Oracle shares were also down in Thursday's early trading as investors weighed the disruption to its own cloud expansion.

Legal Issues Add to Pressure

The reported cancellation comes amid legal concerns surrounding Super Micro's co-founder Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw. U.S. authorities have accused him of involvement in a scheme to move restricted Nvidia chips to China.

While Liaw pleaded not guilty in a New York courtroom earlier this month, the situation appears to be affecting confidence among enterprise customers.

Beyond the Oracle loss, BlueFin raised red flags regarding Super Micro's excess inventory of older GPU stock, which is becoming harder to sell.

Is SMCI Stock a Hold or Sell Right Now? 

On TipRanks, SMCI stock has a Hold consensus rating based on three Buys, eight Holds, and two Sell ratings. The average Super Micro Computer price target of $30.53 implies 11.60% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, SMCI shares have fallen 11.3%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Sam Bankman-Fried Abandons His FTX Retrial Bid as He Slams the Court for Being Unfair
data.description:
The fallen king of crypto just gave up on his newest escape plan. Sam Bankman-Fried pulled back his request for a brand-new trial because he believes the current system is rigged against him. This move shows that he is changing his legal strategy after being told he must spend 25 years behind bars. The founder of the broken FTX exchange is now choosing to stay in his jail cell while a higher court looks at his larger appeal, rather than pushing for a new hearing that he thinks will fail.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SBF Abandons His New Trial Request

The man who once ran a multi-billion dollar empire is now changing his legal path from behind bars. He told the judge overseeing his case that he no longer wants to push for a new trial right now because he doubts he would receive a "fair hearing" in the current court. This is a massive change from just a few weeks ago when his mother tried to help him restart the case with new evidence. He is now telling the court that he would rather wait until his other legal battles are finished before he tries to ask for a "reset" again.

Bankman-Fried Drafts His Own Defense

Life in a Brooklyn detention center has forced the former billionaire to work without his usual army of experts. He admitted that he "largely drafted the motion himself" because he does not have access to the tools he used to have. His parents helped him organize his thoughts and even printed the papers because he "no longer had access to a word processor" in his cell. This shows a very different side of the man who once had the world at his fingertips. He is now fighting for his freedom with simple tools while serving a quarter-century in prison.

Lawyers Target a Higher Court

His legal team is putting all of its energy into a much bigger fight that is already moving forward. They are trying to convince a higher court that the original trial was "fundamentally unfair" to the FTX founder. They believe the judge at that time placed too many limits on what Bankman-Fried could say to the jury. If this higher court agrees with them, it could change his entire future. For now, he is staying focused on this one big chance instead of wasting time on smaller requests that he believes will lead to a dead end.

The Judge Keeps the 25-Year Lock

Even though he is changing his plans, the prison doors remain firmly shut. Bankman-Fried is still serving 25 years for seven different counts of fraud and conspiracy that led to the death of FTX in 2022. This decision to pull back his request shows that he is trying to be very smart about his next move. He does not want to lose a hearing today if he can try again later when things might go his way. He remains locked away while the world watches his slow and difficult battle for freedom.

This latest legal shift arrives as the broader market finds its footing again. Over the past seven days, the crypto world has been flooded with green as major assets like Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and Ethereum (ETH-USD) climb toward new milestones, giving investors a reason to stay optimistic.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: 'Load Up Before Earnings,' Says Top Investor About Alphabet Stock
data.description:
It's that time again, as Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) prepares to unveil its latest quarterly filing next week on April 29. There has been a flurry of activity over the past few months, and investors are eagerly anticipating the opportunity to peek behind the curtain.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Indeed, there has been no shortage of developments impacting the market at large and the tech sector in particular. Many have placed a damper over the market, as fighting in the Middle East and worries over capex spending have pressured share prices.

Alphabet was no exception, and GOOG declined throughout the months of February and March. April has been another story, however, and the company benefited from the rising sentiment that flowed from prospects of a ceasefire.

Geopolitics aside, the rapidly developing AI technology – and Alphabet's place in the ecosystem – will be among the prime topics on next week's earnings call. Top investor Michael Del Monte is expecting to find plenty of reasons to feel confident in the company's prospects next week.

"I believe Alphabet may be in a position of strength among the hyperscalers in terms of AI development and adoption," states the 5-star investor, who is among the top 1% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Del Monte points out that the company was quite busy over the past few months. He notes that Alphabet closed its acquisition of Wiz, deepened its partnership with Broadcom, and has continued to make advancements with its AI development.

The investor is particularly encouraged by the collaboration between Alphabet and Broadcom to develop up to 3.5 GW of TPU capacity for Anthropic in the years ahead. He posits that this deal secures the company's leadership as the custom silicon provider for third-party customers.

Moreover, Del Monte also cites the many strides the company is making with its AI models, such as the recent release of Gemma 4 (built on Gemini 3) and Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 in the physical AI realm. The Wiz acquisition should help shore up the company's cybersecurity across its AI platforms, he adds.

"Advancements in AI development could compound growth over the coming years, particularly for agentic AI, AI inferencing, and physical AI," emphasizes Del Monte, who reiterates a Strong Buy rating for GOOG. (To watch Del Monte's track record, click here)

That's the spirit on Wall Street as well. With 11 Buys and 1 Hold, GOOG enjoys a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $385.44 points to an upside of 14%. (See GOOG stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs May Add $3T, But Nervous Investors Say 'Show me the Profits!'
data.description:
It might sound odd with trillions of dollars of combined market value set to join U.S. stock markets with expected SpaceX, Open AI and Anthropic IPOs, but nervous investors are asking these tech titans to 'show me the profits.'

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

$3T in Market Value

According to LPL Financial estimates, the trio could add $3 trillion to the more than $69 million U.S. equity market. This would mark the largest wave of IPOs in history, but it would also be unprecedented because all three firms are losing money. There is little track record of sustained earnings from these tech tyros.

Investors, according to Reuters analysis, are beginning to ask what this all means for those getting set to take a stake.

"Once we move past that excitement stage where everybody wants to own it, it's going to be really critical for these companies to show exactly what their profits are," said Anthony Saglimbene, chief market strategist at Ameriprise.

Rodney Comegys, chief investment officer at Vanguard Capital Management, added: "History shows that not every early leader in a new technology ends up being a long-term winner, which is why diversified exposure remains important."

Valuable and Unprofitable

Elon Musk's space and AI firm SpaceX is targeting a $1.75 trillion valuation in a potential June listing. Iit would be the largest IPO ever. OpenAI is reportedly seeking a valuation of around $1 trillion and rival Anthropic was valued at $380 billion in a February funding round.

SpaceX, which is planning an IPO as early as June, posted a nearly $5 billion loss on more than $18.6 billion of revenue last year, according to excerpts of the company's confidential registration statement reviewed by Reuters. OpenAI and rival Anthropic are in early-stage preparations for their IPOs and are also unprofitable said the Reuters piece.

"Investors often latch onto a story where the potential growth is vast, and certain people might believe SpaceX has what it takes to go to infinity and beyond. While there is certainly a buzz around the company, there are also risks," said Dan Coatsworth, head of markets at AJ Bell. "It is imperative that investors do not get carried away, and that they weigh up the pros and cons when considering any investments, even ones that sound so exciting."

He said investors could take advantage if asset managers get too nervous post-IPO. "Retail investors might already be salivating at the prospect of buying stock, and there is a queue of index tracker funds waiting to invest following 'fast entry' changes to index qualification rules. However, there is also a large group of venture capital funds, asset managers and staff who may already be sitting on significant gains if the IPO goes well, and who might want to cash out quickly," he said.

What Other IPOs are on the Way?

Let's look at what other IPOs are on the way with our TipRanks IPO calendar.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Dow Jones Loses Steam on Unexpected Jobless Claims Data
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Thursday in the red following the release of new jobs data from the Department of Labor.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

For the week ended April 18, initial jobless claims rose by 6,000 to 214,000, above the consensus estimate of 210,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, rose by 12,000 to 1.821 million and topped the expectation of 1.816 million. Initial jobless claims serve as a gauge of layoffs, while continuing jobless claims track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits and reflect ongoing unemployment.

*This is a developing story. Stay tuned for additional updates.*
data.author: Eddie Pan
